div.head: The Ambassador in Japan (Grew) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 1249. Department’s 506, November 27, 8 p.m. I called this afternoon on the Minister for Foreign Affairs and made the desired approach fully as instructed. The Minister said that he was unaware of any new Japanese demands for facilities in Indochina including (a) naval and air bases both on the Tongking Gulf and along Indochina’s southern seacoast and (b) control of Saigon, but that he would investigate and would let me know the result of his inquiry as soon as possible. I clearly emphasized the inevitable reaction in the United States if the report should prove to be accurate.
